title: Flipping Out for Pineapple Upside-Down Cake Day
story: Bakers everywhere flip their world upside-down on April 20 for National Pineapple Upside-Down Cake Day! This topsy-turvy treat gets its name because you bake it backwards - the pineapple rings and cherries go on the bottom of the pan first, then you pour cake batter on top. When it's done baking, you flip the whole pan over like magic! The fruit becomes a beautiful golden topping that looks like sunshine. Families gather in kitchens to make this classic dessert together, and social media fills with photos of perfectly flipped cakes. Some brave bakers even try creative twists with different fruits!
activity_question: What makes pineapple upside-down cake special compared to regular cake?
activity_choices: You bake it backwards with fruit on the bottom first
It's made with only pineapple juice
You eat it while standing on your head
It's baked in a special upside-down oven
activity_answer: You bake it backwards with fruit on the bottom first
bonus_fact: The cake became popular in the 1920s when canned pineapple made it easy to make year-round!

title: Lima Beans Get Their Day!
story: Lima beans finally get the respect they deserve on April 20! This green vegetable often gets pushed around dinner plates, but today people celebrate lima beans by cooking them in soups, stews, and casseroles. Some families even have lima bean taste tests to find new ways to enjoy them.
activity_question: How do people typically celebrate National Lima Bean Respect Day?
activity_choices: By cooking lima beans in different dishes
By planting lima bean gardens only
activity_answer: By cooking lima beans in different dishes
bonus_fact: Lima beans are also called butter beans in some places!

title: Fries Get Cheesy on National Cheddar Fries Day
story: Golden french fries get smothered in gooey, melted cheddar cheese every April 20 for National Cheddar Fries Day! Restaurants across the country celebrate by offering special deals on this crispy, cheesy combo. Food trucks pile on extra cheese, diners create towering portions, and families make homemade versions in their kitchens. Some places get creative with different cheddar types - from sharp white cheddar to smoky aged varieties. Social media fills with drool-worthy photos of cheese-covered fries. Whether you dip your fries in cheese sauce or have it melted right on top, this holiday proves that everything tastes better with cheddar!
activity_question: What do restaurants often do to celebrate National Cheddar Fries Day?
activity_choices: Offer special deals on cheddar fries
activity_answer: Offer special deals on cheddar fries
bonus_fact: Some restaurants use up to five different types of cheddar cheese on their fries!

title: Speaking of Amazing: Chinese Language Day
story: Every April 20, people around the world celebrate Chinese Language Day! This special day honors one of the most spoken languages on Earth. Schools teach students how to write beautiful Chinese characters with brushes and ink. Libraries host storytelling sessions in Mandarin and Cantonese. Social media fills with people sharing their favorite Chinese words and phrases. It's a perfect time to learn "ni hao" (hello) or discover how Chinese writing looks like tiny works of art!
activity_question: What do people use to write Chinese characters on Chinese Language Day?
follow_up_question: What Chinese word or phrase would you most like to learn?
